title: podman run fails to start container when SELinux security label is specified
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When a container is started using `podman run --security-opt "label=level:s0:110"` option, the container fails to start.

When the same option is specified in Docker compose file and is started using `docker-compose` command, the container starts properly with correct SELinux label.
```
    security_opt:
    - label=level:s0:c110
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
`podman run --security-opt "label=level:s0:110" --name mycont <container-image>`


**Describe the results you received:**
`Error: failed to mount shm tmpfs "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/0893d97ace217715e5d0c399bbbb883b7e93d8f9f85faa70a32fcfe360a05503/userdata/shm": invalid argument`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container should have started with SELinux label as specified in the security option.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Consistently

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.0-dev
API Version:  3.3.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.5
Git Commit:   6678385abc34521dc85a0a549ee306b73ebc2911
Built:        Wed Jul 21 14:14:44 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Closing this issue as the MCS label had the missing 'c'. With correct usage `podman run --security-opt "label=level:s0:c110" --name mycont <container-image>`, it works.
